content.full_text:
COUNTY COUNSEL
108 COURT STREET . JACKSON, CA 95642-2379 . (2O9)223€366. FAx
December 19, 1990
kc:l ,1 oz l'li'30
John Wal1ace, Esq., Chief Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, #8OO
Sacramento, CA 9 5814
Re: ConfLict of Interest/Request for Advice
Dear Mr. Wallace 3
As the county counsel for Amador county, I have been asked to
furnish an opinion as to lrhether or not two members of the board of
supervisors separatel,y have conflicts 
of interest 
and are
disqualified from participating in the decisj.on on an appeal from
the granting of a use permit by the Amador county planning
comrnj-ssion. I believe r also have a potential conflict of interest
and therefore ask the FPPC for formal written advice pursuant to
subsection 18329(b) of Title 2 of the Cafifornia Requlatory code on
all three of the potential conf l-icts. 
I am authorized by the
supervisors to make this request.
Issue Before the Board of suDervisors
l1r. Kenneth Deaver applied to the county for a use perrni.t for a bed
and breakfast inn on a 4o-acre parcel on Steiner Road in Amador
county (rrDeaver parcelrr hereinafter). 
The planning commission
granted the use perrnit and it has been appealed to the board of
supervisors,
Steiner Road lies in the Shenandoah va1ley of Amador county, a
region predorninantly devoted to the growing of wine grapes. Along
steiner Road in the ironediate vicinity of the Deaver parcel are
four wineries, thro of which are Santino Winery and Spinetta winery.
The chief executive officer of Santino Winery is Scott Harvey who
also Lives and operates a vineyard on Steiner Road. The owner of
Spinetta Winery is Charles Spinetta. Mr. Spinetta also operates
fron his winery a wine distribution conpany and has an adjoiningt
vineyard. on the Deaver parcel is a house, where the bed and
breakfast operation would take p1ace, and a refurbished building
originally built as a winery and no!, used to warehouse cased wine
not owned by Mr. Deaver.
Mr. Deaver has a variety of business interests including the
investing in real property with partners. supervisor Tim Davenport
is an equal co-owner erith Mr. Deaver on five parcels of property
located in Amador, EI Dorado and Tuol-umne Counties. 
None is
rernotely close to the Deaver parcel,, 
The equity in the five
parcels is in excess of $100,000. Supervisor Davenport ohrns no
interest in the Deaver parcel.


Mr. Harvey and Mr. Spinetta anong many others are vehemently
opposed to the proposed use of the Deaver parcel for reasons
centerinq around (a) an expected adverse impact caused by the bed
and breakfast inn on their vineyard operations and (b) increased
traffic 
on steiner Road. They argue that vineyard operations such
as sulphuring and other farming practices some of which are
customarily done at night will be incompatible with a bed and
breakfast inn and ultinately will curtail some farroing practices of
the grape growers increasing their costs and winery operators'
costs. They befieve increased traffic 
from Mr. Deaver's proposed
use will be bad for their homes and their wineries.
I am a general partner in two vineyards in the Shenandoah val-ley
one of qrhich is on steiner Road about one-quarter mile away from
the Deaver parcel. My partner and I have no positj,on regarding the
bed and breakfast operation. However, part of the partnerships'
operation includes having wine nade from our grapes for sale to the
public. cases of our wine are lrarehoused at tlr. Deaver's warehouse
on the Deaver parcel for which we pay several hundred dol]ars a
year to I"1r. Deaver. AIso, the partnerships sell grapes to Santino
Winery and recei,ve more than ten thousand doll-ars per year
therefrom. 
The partnerships have ernployed I{r. Spinetta to
distribute their wine, wj.th revenue to the partnerships of several
thousand dollars yearly.
In addition, another supervisor, l,[r. Tom Bamert, is a director and
a shareholder (about 3*) of a local bank. DIr. Deaver is a director
and a shareholder of the same bank. llr. Deaver is a borrower and
a depositor of significant proportions at the Iocal bank. 
He
origrinally bought the Deaver parcel fron the bank. It is not kno$rn
if IvIr. Deaver has any debt with the bank secured by the Deaver
parcel. The second supervisor, Mr. Bamert, may have a conflict of
interest because of his and Mr. Deaver's mutual interest in the
bank.
Because of ny partnerships' business interests with Messrs.
Deaver, spinetta, and Harvey (on behalf of Santino Winery) , I
believe that I have a conflict of interest and should recuse myself
from participating in the appeal and on the issuing of opinions
regarding possible conflicts of interest of the two supervisors.
I have one deputy and I do not wish to place hirn in the position of
writing an opinion regarding my own potential conflict of interest.
Therefore, I seek formal written advice on the following three
questions :
1. 
Do I as county counsel have a potential conflict of
interest so as to preclude roe fron issuing an opinion as to the trro
supervisors' possible conflicts 
of 
interest 
and otherwise
participating in the appeal?
2. 
Does Supervisor Davenport have a potential confl-ict of
interest on Mr. Deaver's apPeal?


3. 
Does Supervisor Banert have a potential conflict 
of
interest on !1r. Deaver,s appeal?
Thank you very much (in advance) for providing formal written
advice on these issues. I request that your advice be forthconing
as soon as possible because the appeal will be heard in January.
County counsel
JFH/P
Yours very tru1y7
